body_txt: There's lots of jobs out there in the world. And jobs need people to fill the positions. Seems simple enough. However, people aren't always qualified for the jobs that they apply for. In theory, someone conducting interviews is supposed to catch the unqualified candidates and filter them out. Practice and theory seldom have anything in common, however. Somehow, these characters wound up in jobs that they clearly had no business being in. A character who is frequently dressed in Stripperiffic fashion being appointed the head of a committee on public decency, or someone who is more than just Hollywood Pudgy working as a diet councilor, a person with claustrophobia being assigned to command a submarine, etc. Sometimes it was being well-connected that got them the job. Other times it was simply that no one else wanted the job, and it had to be filled. And in some cases they just got Kicked Upstairs so that someone else had to deal with them. Also common is the character partaking in Sleeping with the Boss . It could also be a result of The Peter Principle on display. A Very Fake Résumé and/or a Phony Degree might have had something to do with how they ended up in that position. This will frequently overlap with a Pointy-Haired Boss , though in some cases the people in charge were at least wise enough to never put these characters in a position of authority. This is often lampshaded when the character making the appointment will ask " Who better for the job than X?" " only for someone who knows the nominee well and dreads being under their authority to suggest "Literally anybody else!" In some cases, the person assigned the job will be painfully aware of their lack of qualifications. Either they wanted the job for the power, which they intend to use and abuse, as swiftly and frequently as possible, or, barring that, they will find a competent underling to tackle the workload for them while they take the credit. In a very few cases, the person will actually manage to grow into the job and gain the competence they lacked before. A common subversion of this trope is to have someone appear incompetent, only to reveal that they were either Obfuscating Stupidity to fly below the radar, or that they're in fact a Bunny-Ears Lawyer who, despite their eccentricities that seemingly disqualify them, are more than up for the task. May overlap with or be the result of Closest Thing We Got . Compare Deaf Composer . Sucksessor is closely related, when a replacement for someone else is not only inadequate, but incompetent to boot. For examples where the unqualified person is selected to be The Hero , that's The Poorly Chosen One . Sub-Trope to Wrong Line of Work . Super-Trope to The Astronaut Who Shouldn't Be . As questions about qualifications can be controversial, particularly where politics and political appointments are involved, No Real Life Examples, Please! noreallife
